rulings.Case Title: ROOSEVELT HOLLINS et al VS. A.W.CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 23, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT M. SLAYEN AND ASSOCIATES, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Triable issue of fact regarding exposure. Motion for summary adjudication as to strict liability claim is granted. =(302/HEK/JW)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT PECHAR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, OCTOBER 23, 2008. LINE 18. DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING EXPOSURE AND ALSO DEFENDANT FAILED TO MEET BURDEN OF PRODUCTION ON GOVERNMENT CONTRACTOR DEFENSE. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO FALSE REPRESENTATION AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DENIED AS TO REMAINING ISSUES. =(302/HEK/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RENE FERNANDO VS. VIOLET ELIZABETH GRAYSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 23, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT VIOLET GRAYSON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. 1st, 7th and 8th causes of action are not cognizable because the first and second Jet Star actions are not the same and plaintiff was disbarred prior to the filing of the second action. The 2nd, 3rd, 4th and 6th causes of action are time-barred. CCP 340.6. The fifth cause of action is barred due to lack of legally cognizable reliance on the alleged fraud. =(302/HEK/VC)
